text:
Once upon a time, in the lush forest of Whispering Oaks, there was a wise old owl named Olive. Each dawn, Olive would flap her wings vigorously, eager to feast on the rich fresh eggs her niece Daisy laid each morning. One peculiar spring morning, however, Olive heard strange hooting sounds outside her nest. Intrigued, she flew out to investigate.

There was a young squirrel named Spike. Not your ordinary little jumper. Spike claimed he could fly. Indeed, he could! He danced through the branches, teasing the other squirrels, proclaiming he had wings.

Curiosity got the better of Diamond-back rattlesnakes. They noticed Spike's airiness and decided to challenge him. In a forest-wide competition, Spike proved he wasn't just a playful chatterer – he could actually glide. He soared elegantly from bush to bush with leaves dangling from his feet.

Olive admired Spike’s ability from her perch. She decided to teach him some owl wisdom. She
